minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby 2026 property tax rates set — check your bill
minutes_briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has passed Bylaw 14814, which sets the property tax rates for 2026. This bylaw (a local law) sets a 'mill rate' (the amount of tax you pay for every $1,000 of your property's assessed value) for different types of properties, like homes, businesses, and industry. The rates are broken down into five municipal services (like community safety and parks) plus a regional district rate and a special rate for businesses in the Hastings Business Improvement Area (BIA). Your total property tax bill will be calculated by multiplying your property's assessed value by these rates.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review your 2026 property tax notice from Burnaby to confirm the correct mill rate was applied to your property class.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 property tax rates take effect

minutes_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Hotel & Accommodation
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Bylaw No. 14814 
Page 1 
 
CITY OF BURNABY 
BYLAW NO. 14814 
 
A BYLAW to establish tax rates upon all taxable 
land and improvements pursuant to 
sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter 
  
WHEREAS Council must before the 15th day of May, subject to the Community 
Charter, adopt a bylaw to impose property value taxes for the year by establishing tax rates on 
all taxable land and improvements according to their assessed value to provide the money 
required for the purposes specified in section 197 of the Community Charter; and 
 
WHEREAS Council may impose a local service tax in relation to a business 
improvement area on land or improvements, or both, in accordance with section 216 of the 
Community Charter   
 
  
NOW THEREFORE the Council of the City of Burnaby ENACTS as follows: 
  
1. 
This Bylaw may be cited as BURNABY RATES BYLAW 2026. 
  
2. 
Property value taxes at the following tax rates are hereby imposed and levied for the year 
2026: 
 
(a) 
to provide for the municipal purposes set out in Schedule 1 of this 
Bylaw based on the assessed value of land and improvements 
taxable for municipal purposes, in accordance with the rates set out 
in Schedule 1 of this Bylaw;  
 
(b) 
to provide for regional district purposes based on the assessed value 
of land and improvements taxable for such purposes, in accordance 
with the rates set out in Schedule 2 of this Bylaw; and 

Bylaw No. 14814 
Page 2 
 
 
(c) 
to provide for business improvement area local area service tax 
purposes for the Hastings BIA, as defined in Burnaby Business 
Improvement Area (Hastings) Bylaw 2024 (Bylaw No. 14638), 
based on the assessed value of land and improvements taxable for 
such purposes, in accordance with the rate set out in Schedule 3 of 
this Bylaw. 
 
 
  
Read a first time this 28th day of April, 2026 
Read a second time this 28th day of April, 2026 
Read a third time this 28th day of April, 2026           
Reconsidered and adopted this 12th day of May, 2026 
 
 
 
MAYOR 
  
  
DEPUTY CORPORATE OFFICER 
Signed Original Bylaw on file. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

Bylaw No. 14814 
Page 3
